title: mjst CLI config
description:
Generate TypeScript parsers and type definitions from JSON Schemas.

You can supply options via CLI flags or a JSON config file. CLI flags always take precedence over config file values.
$comment: Supply options either via CLI flags or a JSON config file — both approaches are shown below.

schema.description: Path to the schema to process. With the default 'json' input this is a JSON Schema file that is read and parsed. With any other input format it is a JS/TS module that exports a schema, which is loaded and converted to JSON Schema via the matching adapter.

input.description: Source format of the schema. 'json' (default) reads a JSON Schema file directly. Any other format loads 'schema' as a module and converts it to JSON Schema with the matching adapter. Currently implemented: 'typebox', 'zod' (requires zod v4+ in your project).

export.description: Which export of the schema module to use when 'input' is not 'json'. Defaults to the default export, or the sole named export when the module has exactly one.

outDir.description: Output directory for generated TypeScript files. The directory is created automatically if it does not exist. Subdirectories are created as needed when a generated file includes a nested path.

typesOnly.description: Generate only TypeScript type definitions without parser functions. Useful when you only need the type shapes and do not need runtime validation.

build.description: Compile the generated TypeScript files to .js and .d.ts output. A temporary tsconfig is written to the output directory, tsc is invoked, and the intermediate .ts source files are removed when compilation succeeds.

logWarnings.description: Emit a console.warn in the generated parsers for every input key that is not declared in the schema's properties. Useful for detecting schema drift or unexpected data shapes at runtime.

strict.description: Generate parsers that throw on type/shape mismatches (wrong type, missing required property, enum/pattern/min/max violations) instead of coercing invalid input to default values. Unknown extra keys are still allowed.

helpers.description: Controls how generated parsers reference their runtime helpers. 'package' emits imports from @amritk/helpers (requires it to be installed in the consumer project). 'embedded' ships the helper source under outDir/_helpers/ so the output is self-contained. When omitted, the CLI auto-detects: it picks 'package' if @amritk/helpers resolves from outDir, otherwise 'embedded'.

config.description: Path to a JSON config file. Keys match the option names in this schema (schema, outDir, typesOnly, build, logWarnings, strict, helpers). CLI flags take precedence over config file values.

x-example-titles: Log warnings — warn on unknown input properties
